title: dioscorea subtomentosa
intro: Dioscorea subtomentosa, also known as Mexican wild yam, is a type of perennial vine native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to stimulate the body's natural production of progesterone. The health benefits of Dioscorea subtomentosa are numerous and include relief from menstrual cramps, menopausal symptoms, and anxiety, as well as support for immune function and cardiovascular health. Dioscorea subtomentosa is often prepared in herbal supplements, teas, and tinctures, although its root is also used in traditional folk medicine to make a variety of preparations, including capsules and topical creams.
